originalTitle: South Africa's top court bars repeat asylum applications
title: Fair Rules for Everyone in Sunny South Africa!
content:
Hello, amazing friends all over Africa! It's your kid-friendly reporter, Kojo, here with some super important news from a beautiful country called South Africa. We're going to talk about some big news that helps make sure rules are fair for everyone. It's about people who need a safe place to live and how South Africa helps them.

What is a Safe Place?

Imagine you live in a place where things are not safe. Maybe there's a big problem, like no food, or people are not being kind to each other. It can be very scary. Sometimes, people have to leave their homes and look for a new, safe place to live. These brave people are often called 'refugees'. They are looking for peace and a new beginning. Did you know? Millions of people around the world have had to leave their homes because of danger or problems. Countries like South Africa try to help them.

Asking for Help: What is Asylum?

When people need a new safe home, they can go to another country and ask for 'asylum'. This is like asking for special permission to stay there because you are not safe in your own country. It's a very serious request. Countries like South Africa have special rules to help these people. They listen to their stories and decide if they can stay. Think of it like this: if your house was not safe, you would want a kind friend to let you stay at their house, right? Asylum is a bit like that, but with whole countries!

Big Judges and Important Decisions

In South Africa, just like in many countries, there are very important people called judges. They work in a special place called a 'court'. These judges listen to difficult problems and make big decisions about rules and laws. Their job is to make sure everything is fair and right for everyone. South Africa has a 'top court'. This means it's the most important group of judges in the whole country! When they make a rule, everyone must follow it. Did you know? The highest court in a country is often called the Supreme Court or Constitutional Court. They deal with the most important rules of the land!

A New Rule from the Top Judges

So, what happened in South Africa? The top judges made a new rule. This rule is about people who ask for asylum. Sometimes, a person might ask to stay in South Africa. If their request is not accepted, it means the judges said 'no' this time. Before, some people would ask again and again, even if their first request was denied. They would keep trying many times. The new rule says: if your request for asylum is not accepted, you cannot just keep asking again and again. You can't make 'repeat applications'. It's like when you ask your parent for a treat. If they say 'no' after you ask nicely, you shouldn't ask every minute, right? You respect their decision.

Why This New Rule is Important

A very important person in the South African government, called the 'Home Affairs Minister', was very happy about this new rule. He said it was a 'victory'. A victory is like a win! He feels it's a win for fairness and for the way South Africa helps people in need. The Minister said that some people were 'abusing' the system. What does 'abuse' mean? It means using something in a bad or unfair way. Imagine you have a special game with rules. If some players don't follow the rules and try to cheat, it makes the game not fair for everyone else. So, some people who didn't truly need asylum were asking again and again. This made it harder for the people who really, truly needed help to get it quickly. Did you know? When rules are fair, it helps everyone. It means that the people who need help the most can get it without long delays.

Making Things Fair for Everyone

This new rule means that the process for asking for asylum will be fairer and faster. It helps make sure that the special help South Africa offers goes to those who truly need it. It also means that the people who work hard to process these requests can do their job better, without being held up by many repeat applications. So, this big decision from South Africa's top judges is all about making sure that the rules are clear, fair, and help everyone. It’s a good step for a country that wants to help people in the right way. That's all the news for now from sunny South Africa! Keep being kind to each other, and remember that fair rules help us all. This is Kojo, your kid reporter, signing off! Bye for now!
summary: South Africa's top judges made a new rule that says people cannot ask to live there many times if their first try was not accepted, to make sure the rules are fair for everyone.
whyMatters: This new rule helps make sure that people who truly need help get it, and that everyone plays by the same fair rules, so the system works better for everyone.

originalTitle: Uganda's president sworn in for record seventh term
title: Big News from Uganda! Our President Stays to Lead!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your friendly kid reporter here, bringing you super exciting news from our wonderful continent, Africa! Today, we're zooming our attention to a beautiful country called Uganda. It's a place with green hills, big lakes, and super friendly people!

Meet Our President!

Guess what? The leader of Uganda, a very important person called President Yoweri Museveni, just made a big promise. This promise means he will continue to be the president for more years! It's like when your head teacher promises to keep leading your school to make it the best it can be. President Museveni is 81 years old. Wow! That's older than most of our grandpas and grandmas! He has been leading Uganda for a very, very long time. Can you imagine?

What Does 'Sworn In' Mean?

When we say President Museveni was 'sworn in', it means he stood in front of many people and made a very special promise. He promised to take good care of Uganda and all its people. It's like making a solemn vow to be responsible and do your best work. This big promise means he will continue his job as president. He will keep helping Uganda grow and stay safe. It's a very important day for the country! **Did you know?** Being a president is like being the captain of a very big ship – the whole country! They help make sure everyone is safe and happy, and that the country sails in the right direction.

A Super Long Time!

President Museveni has been the leader of Uganda for about 40 years! That's more years than your mom or dad has been alive for many of you! It's a really, really long time to be in charge. Imagine being the head of your school for 40 years! You would see so many children grow up. Because he's been president for so long, he has seen many changes in Uganda. He has seen children like you grow up and become adults. He has seen new buildings built and new roads made. This time, he is starting his seventh term. That means he has been chosen seven times to be the president. That's a lot of times! It's like winning the same special race seven times in a row!

How Does a President Get Chosen?

How does someone become a president? Well, in many countries, people get to choose their leader. This is called an 'election'. Imagine if everyone in your class got to vote for who they wanted to be the class monitor. That's a bit like an election! In Uganda, big people, like your parents and older brothers and sisters, went to special places to vote. They put a mark next to the name of the person they wanted to be president. President Museveni got the most votes. Some people talked a lot about the election, and not everyone agreed with the results. But in the end, President Museveni was chosen to lead again. It's a bit like when you play a game, and sometimes there are different ideas about who won, but a decision is made. **Did you know?** In an election, every grown-up gets one chance to vote. This helps to make sure that everyone's voice can be heard when choosing a leader!

Uganda: A Land of Beauty!

Uganda is a truly special country in Africa. It's sometimes called the 'Pearl of Africa' because it's so beautiful! It has big lakes, like Lake Victoria, which is one of the biggest lakes in the whole world. It also has tall mountains and amazing animals. You can find mountain gorillas in Uganda, which are super rare and special! They live high up in the mountains. And guess what? The River Nile, one of the longest rivers in the world, starts its long journey right there in Uganda! **Did you know?** Uganda has many different languages spoken by its people, but English is one of the official languages, just like in many other African countries!

What Does a President Do for You?

So, what does a president like President Museveni do for children like us? A president works very hard to make sure there are schools for everyone. He also helps make sure there are doctors and nurses to keep us healthy. They also work to make sure there's enough food for everyone and that our communities are safe places to play and grow. They want to see children get a good education and have bright futures. Every day, the president and their team make important decisions that affect how the country grows and how everyone lives. It's a very big job with lots of responsibilities! **Did you know?** Every child in the world has special rights, like the right to go to school, the right to play, and the right to be safe. Presidents and leaders help make sure these rights are protected!

A Bright Future for Uganda!

So, there you have it! President Museveni will continue to lead Uganda. It's a big moment for the country. We hope that with his leadership, Uganda will continue to be a wonderful place for all its children to grow up, learn, and shine! Keep exploring and learning about our amazing continent, Africa! Until next time, stay curious and kind!
summary: President Yoweri Museveni of Uganda has been sworn in for his seventh term, meaning he will continue to lead the country, a job he has done for about 40 years, after winning recent elections.
whyMatters: This is important because President Museveni has led Uganda for a very long time, and he will continue to make big decisions for the country, affecting schools, health, and safety for everyone, especially children.

title: Ethiopian marathon runner Melese dies aged 36
content: Ethiopian long-distance runner Yebrgual Melese dies at the age of 36 after falling ill during a routine training session.

originalTitle: No sign of larger hantavirus outbreak, says UN health agency
title: Keeping Away Tiny Germs: What Big Doctors Say!
content:
Hello, Super Kids of Africa!

Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you super important news from our big, beautiful continent. We always want to keep you safe and healthy. Today, we're talking about tiny, tiny things called germs. Germs are so small you can't even see them! But sometimes, they can make us feel a little bit sick. Don't worry, we're learning how to be smart and strong against them!

What Are We Talking About Today?

There's a special health group called the World Health Organization. We call it the WHO for short. Imagine them as a team of super smart doctors and health helpers from all over the world. Their job is to help everyone stay healthy and safe. They watch out for any new sicknesses that might come around. Recently, the WHO was watching a special kind of tiny germ. This germ has a big name: hantavirus. Wow, that's a mouthful! But don't be scared. The WHO says that right now, it's not a big problem that is spreading a lot. This is good news! But it's still good to learn about it. *Did you know?* There are good germs and bad germs! Some germs even help your tummy digest food!

What is Hantavirus?

Hantavirus is a tiny germ. It's so small, you need a special super-seeing machine called a microscope to see it. These tiny germs don't jump from person to person easily. That means if your friend has it, you won't easily catch it just by playing with them. So, where do these germs come from? They usually come from small animals like mice and rats. Yes, those little critters with long tails! The germs can be found in their wee-wee (urine), their poo-poo (droppings), and their spit (saliva). When these dry up, the tiny germ dust can float in the air. If someone breathes in this dusty air, they can sometimes get sick. This is why we must always be careful around these animals. *Did you know?* Mice and rats are very good at hiding. They love dark, quiet places!

What Does 'Outbreak' Mean?

The WHO talked about an 'outbreak.' What does that mean? Imagine if you and many of your friends all got the sniffles at the same time. That would be like a small sniffle outbreak! An 'outbreak' means that more people than usual are getting sick from the same thing. The WHO always watches very carefully to see if there is an outbreak happening anywhere in the world. They want to know so they can help people get better and stop more people from getting sick. Right now, for this hantavirus, the WHO says there is **no big outbreak**. This means that not many people are getting sick from it in a way that is spreading very fast. This is excellent news for all of us. It means we can relax a little, but still be smart and careful. *Did you know?* When you sneeze or cough, tiny water drops fly out. That's why we cover our mouths with our elbow!

How Can We Stay Safe?

Being safe from germs is super easy and fun! Here are some simple things you can do: 1. **Wash Your Hands!** This is the most important thing! After you play outside, before you eat, and after you use the toilet, wash your hands with soap and water. Scrub them well for as long as it takes to sing 'Happy Birthday' twice! Washing hands sends those pesky germs far, far away. 2. **Keep Food Safe!** Make sure all your food is in closed containers. Don't leave food out where mice or rats can find it. Remember, these little animals love to look for yummy snacks! 3. **Clean Up!** Help your grown-ups keep your home and school clean. If you see any mess or crumbs on the floor, tell a grown-up. Mice and rats love messy places because they can find food and good hiding spots there. Cleaning up helps to keep them away. 4. **Tell a Grown-Up!** If you ever see a mouse or a rat, don't touch it! Tell a grown-up right away. Grown-ups know how to safely deal with these animals. *Did you know?* Handwashing with soap and water is like having a superhero shield against many germs!

Why is This Important for Us in Africa?

Even though the news might be about places far away, it's super important for us here in Africa too! Why? Because healthy habits keep everyone safe, no matter where they live. We have mice and rats in many places, just like other countries. So, learning about hygiene and being careful is a super skill for everyone, everywhere. Keeping our homes and villages clean means we can play more, learn more, and just be happier and healthier. We don't want any tiny germs making us feel bad when we could be running and laughing and playing! *Did you know?* Africa is home to many amazing animals, but wild rats are not the friendly kind of animal we want inside our homes!

Let's Be Super Clean Heroes!

So, my fantastic friends, the big news from the WHO is good news right now. No big hantavirus outbreak! But it's also a reminder for us to always be super clean and careful. Remember your handwashing, help keep things tidy, and tell a grown-up if you see any unwelcome animal visitors. By doing these simple things, you are being a super health hero! You are protecting yourself, your family, and your friends from tiny germs. Let's all work together to stay healthy, strong, and ready for adventure every single day!
summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) says there is no big spreading of a sickness called hantavirus right now, which is good news, but it's important to keep clean and stay safe from tiny germs carried by rats and mice.
whyMatters: This matters because staying clean and knowing about germs helps us all stay healthy and strong, especially since tiny animals like rats can carry sicknesses, and we want to keep everyone in Africa safe and well.

originalTitle: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
content: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".

originalTitle: 'I'm not going anywhere' - furious Real president Perez calls for elections
title: A Big Boss and a Big Team! Real Madrid's Leader Makes a Special Call!
content:
Hello, amazing kids! Welcome to your special news from Africa! Today, we have a story about a very, very famous football team and its super important boss. This team is called Real Madrid. Maybe you have seen their white shirts on TV? Or maybe your big brother or sister talks about them? They are from a country called Spain, very far away from us. But many, many people in Africa love Real Madrid football! It's a team that many of you might cheer for!

What Happened with Real Madrid?

Something important happened with Real Madrid. The boss of the team, his name is Mr. Florentino Perez. He had a special meeting where many grown-ups asked him questions. This meeting is called a "press conference." Imagine your teacher asking you questions, but with lots and lots of people! Mr. Perez seemed a little bit unhappy. He was talking in a strong voice with the people who were asking questions. These grown-ups are called journalists. Journalists are people who write news stories for everyone to read in newspapers or show on TV.

Who is Mr. Florentino Perez?

Mr. Florentino Perez is like the super-duper captain of a big ship, but instead of a ship, he leads Real Madrid, the football team! He is the big boss. He makes many big decisions for the team. He decides who plays, who coaches, and how the team should be run. It's a very important job! Imagine being the boss of your favorite football team. Wow, that would be amazing, right? Mr. Perez has been the boss for a long, long time. He loves Real Madrid very much. He always wants them to be the best team in the whole wide world! Did you know? Real Madrid is one of the oldest and most famous football teams in the world! They have won many, many big shiny cups over the years!

What is a "Press Conference"?

A "press conference" is like a special meeting. Imagine all the grown-ups who write news for newspapers and TV, all in one room. They all want to ask questions to a very important person. In this story, the important person is Mr. Florentino Perez, the big boss of Real Madrid. He stands in front of them and answers their questions. Sometimes, everyone is happy and calm. But sometimes, like in this story, things can get a little bit, well, not so happy. When we say it was "tense," it means people were not calm. They might have been a bit worried or unhappy. Mr. Perez was speaking strongly because he felt strongly about his team and what he believes in. Did you know? News reporters from all over the world go to press conferences to get important news for you and your family!

Why No Trophies is a Big Deal

Last year, Real Madrid played many, many football games. They ran very fast, they kicked the ball super hard, and they scored! But at the end of the whole year, they did not win any big shining trophies. A "trophy" is like a super big cup or a special medal that you get when you win a competition. Imagine playing in a school sports day and not winning any ribbon or medal. It makes you a little sad, right? For a team as famous as Real Madrid, not winning any trophies in a whole year is a very, very big deal. It made some fans, and maybe even Mr. Perez, a bit sad or worried. They always want to be champions and hold up those shiny cups! Did you know? Winning trophies is very important for big football teams. It shows everyone they are strong and play the best football!

What are "Elections"?

Now, this is where our story gets super interesting! Because Real Madrid did not win any trophies last year, some people started to wonder if maybe they needed a new boss. So, Mr. Perez said something very big. He said, "Let's have 'elections' for the boss!" What are elections? Imagine your class needs to choose a new class captain. Everyone gets to vote, right? You choose who you think will be the best leader. That's what elections are! It means that people who are members of Real Madrid will get to choose who they want to be the boss of their favourite team. Mr. Perez wants people to vote and choose if he should still be the boss, or if someone new should take his place. He believes he is still the best boss, and he is saying, "I'm not going anywhere!" He wants to keep leading his team and help them win again. Did you know? In many countries in Africa, people vote in elections to choose their leaders, like a president or a mayor! It's how people choose who will lead.

Why This Matters for Football Fans

Why is this story important? Because football is loved by so many people, especially here in Africa! Many of you might dream of playing football one day, just like your heroes. Real Madrid is a team that inspires many young players all over the world. What happens to their boss and their team can make many people happy or sad. Mr. Perez is showing that even when things are tough, a leader needs to be strong and stand up for what they believe in. He believes he is the right person to lead Real Madrid back to winning many, many shiny trophies! This story shows us how important good leadership is, even in sports! Everyone wants their team to be the best. This is about choosing the right person to make that happen. It's about teamwork, even for the boss. Football is called "the beautiful game" because so many people love to watch it and play it all around the world! So, that's our story about Mr. Perez and Real Madrid. Even big teams have big decisions to make. We hope Real Madrid finds a way to win lots of trophies again, and that Mr. Perez stays strong! Keep loving football, kids! See you next time for more amazing news from Africa!
summary: The boss of the famous Real Madrid football team, Mr. Florentino Perez, was unhappy about his team not winning trophies last year and called for elections so people can choose if he should stay as boss.
whyMatters: This story is important because many kids in Africa love football and Real Madrid, and it shows how even big teams need good leaders to help them win.
